programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Atlantic City is in for a fish-lift. Ocean Casino Resort wants to bring the ocean into Ocean.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Treasure hunter Drew Pritchard travels the length and breadth of the country in search of elusive gems and other magnificent objects.

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.sub-title: Scuba Lights, Convertible Sandals, Racecar Simulators, and Fiberglass Doors
programme.desc: More everyday items are put under the microscope as we discover how scuba lights are manufactured and convertible sandals are produced.

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.sub-title: Wood Windows, Woven Cashmere, Plastic Recyclers, and Architectural Glass
programme.desc: More everyday items are put under the microscope as we discover how wood windows are manufactured and woven cashmere is produced.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike buys a 1995 Ford Escort RS Cosworth, a rare vehicle originally designed to qualify as a car for the World Rally Championship, and Ant attempts to bring back the 227hp engine's famous 'fizz'.

programme.title: Tanked
programme.sub-title: Clay Matthews' Green Bay Eel Tank
programme.desc: Clay Matthews from the Green Bay Packers is in the process of building his dream home, and it wouldn't be complete without a custom tank.

programme.title: Kindig Customs
programme.desc: Kevin Hart's Challenger fabrication is complete. Dave takes his futuristic theme into the dash and interior with a new custom colour, flawless bodywork and seamless assembly.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Treasure hunter Drew Pritchard travels the length and breadth of the country in search of elusive gems and other magnificent objects.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers World Tour
programme.desc: Mike and Elvis hunt for Australia's favourite pick up, the Hilux. Despite years of farm use, the boys aim to create the ultimate off-roading beast.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: Tony breaks records with the biggest gold haul in Gold Rush history. Parker battles to save his dream washplant, and Todd is finally on the gold after seven weeks at the Buckland.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike buys a 1995 Ford Escort RS Cosworth, a rare vehicle originally designed to qualify as a car for the World Rally Championship, and Ant attempts to bring back the 227hp engine's famous 'fizz'.

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.sub-title: Scuba Lights, Convertible Sandals, Racecar Simulators, and Fiberglass Doors
programme.desc: More everyday items are put under the microscope as we discover how scuba lights are manufactured and convertible sandals are produced.

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.sub-title: Wood Windows, Woven Cashmere, Plastic Recyclers, and Architectural Glass
programme.desc: More everyday items are put under the microscope as we discover how wood windows are manufactured and woven cashmere is produced.

programme.title: Drive Safer
programme.desc: Kris and Jess explore how vehicle safety has evolved, comparing modern and older cars. Featuring Subaru's latest safety innovations, the episode questions if driver education needs a shake-up to improve road safety.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Treasure hunter Drew Pritchard travels the length and breadth of the country in search of elusive gems and other magnificent objects.

programme.title: Tanked
programme.sub-title: Clay Matthews' Green Bay Eel Tank
programme.desc: Clay Matthews from the Green Bay Packers is in the process of building his dream home, and it wouldn't be complete without a custom tank.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike buys a 1995 Ford Escort RS Cosworth, a rare vehicle originally designed to qualify as a car for the World Rally Championship, and Ant attempts to bring back the 227hp engine's famous 'fizz'.

programme.title: The UnXplained
programme.desc: Are ancient rituals like voodoo and exorcism mere superstition, or do they physically impact our world? Evidence suggests the mind and body may tap into hidden forces that defy scientific explanation.

programme.title: History's Most Shocking
programme.desc: Tony analyzes shocking moments: a woman surviving a tornado, an out-of-control elevator, a motorcyclist swept away by a flood, and the cause of the historic 2004 Thailand tsunami.

programme.title: Lost Gold of World War II
programme.desc: John battles the elements to access a hidden vault while a knife found in the tunnel offers clues to who left it. A promising find at Breach 6 has the team going all in for gold.

programme.title: Hidden Heroes: The Nisei Soldiers of WWII
programme.desc: Featuring sit-down interviews with experts and historians, follows the story of the Japanese American soldiers of WWII who fought for the ideals of American democracy.

programme.title: The UnXplained
programme.desc: Are ancient rituals like voodoo and exorcism mere superstition, or do they physically impact our world? Evidence suggests the mind and body may tap into hidden forces that defy scientific explanation.

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There: Bermuda Triangle Edition
programme.desc: Journalist Tony Harris travels to the Florida coast, a hot spot for UFO sightings. Tony and a team of experts will analyse compelling evidence of unidentified aerial phenomena at the Bermuda Triangle and around the world.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Treasure hunter Drew Pritchard travels the length and breadth of the country in search of elusive gems and other magnificent objects.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike buys a 1995 Ford Escort RS Cosworth, a rare vehicle originally designed to qualify as a car for the World Rally Championship, and Ant attempts to bring back the 227hp engine's famous 'fizz'.

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.sub-title: Scuba Lights, Convertible Sandals, Racecar Simulators, and Fiberglass Doors
programme.desc: More everyday items are put under the microscope as we discover how scuba lights are manufactured and convertible sandals are produced.

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.sub-title: Wood Windows, Woven Cashmere, Plastic Recyclers, and Architectural Glass
programme.desc: More everyday items are put under the microscope as we discover how wood windows are manufactured and woven cashmere is produced.

programme.title: Drive Safer
programme.desc: Kris and Jess explore how vehicle safety has evolved, comparing modern and older cars. Featuring Subaru's latest safety innovations, the episode questions if driver education needs a shake-up to improve road safety.

programme.title: Tanked
programme.sub-title: Clay Matthews' Green Bay Eel Tank
programme.desc: Clay Matthews from the Green Bay Packers is in the process of building his dream home, and it wouldn't be complete without a custom tank.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Treasure hunter Drew Pritchard travels the length and breadth of the country in search of elusive gems and other magnificent objects.

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.sub-title: Gas Barbecues, Mattress Pads, and Ear Prostheses
programme.desc: More everyday items are put under the microscope as we discover how gas barbecues are manufactured and mattress pads are produced.

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.sub-title: Recycled Skateboards, Braided Pastry, Construction Trailers, and Vises
programme.desc: More everyday items are put under the microscope as we discover how skateboards are manufactured and braided pastry is produced.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike hopes to capitalise on '80s nostalgia by restoring an original six-cylinder 1982 Toyota Supra with a tired interior, faulty head gasket, compromised exhaust system, and only one working pop-up headlight.

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Lifestyle brand Salt Life needs Wayde and Brett's help again. This time they want an aquarium even bigger and better than the original.

programme.title: Counting Cars
programme.desc: The shop finally gets to work on Danny's famous '71 Super Fly Cadillac, but Kevin isn't feeling the love. Later, Grandpa wins big at poker but blows a deal for a new motorcycle.

programme.title: Counting Cars
programme.desc: Danny turns a 1967 GTO into a world-class muscle car and hopes for a monstrous flip. Later, Mike and Roli get in a sticky situation, and Danny tries to buy a classic Mercury from an old friend.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Treasure hunter Drew Pritchard travels the length and breadth of the country in search of elusive gems and other magnificent objects.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: Tony breaks records with the biggest gold haul in Gold Rush history. Parker battles to save his dream washplant, and Todd is finally on the gold after seven weeks at the Buckland.

programme.title: Mysteries at the Museum
programme.sub-title: Spaceship on the Beach, Boogie Bomb and Wyatt Earp's Great Escape
programme.desc: Don Wildman investigates an out-of-this-world object that washed up on a beach, the explosive story of how a WWII bomb ended up buried beneath New York City.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike hopes to capitalise on '80s nostalgia by restoring an original six-cylinder 1982 Toyota Supra with a tired interior, faulty head gasket, compromised exhaust system, and only one working pop-up headlight.

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.sub-title: Gas Barbecues, Mattress Pads, and Ear Prostheses
programme.desc: More everyday items are put under the microscope as we discover how gas barbecues are manufactured and mattress pads are produced.

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.sub-title: Recycled Skateboards, Braided Pastry, Construction Trailers, and Vises
programme.desc: More everyday items are put under the microscope as we discover how skateboards are manufactured and braided pastry is produced.

programme.title: Drive Safer
programme.desc: In the final episode, the focus is on safety when purchasing your next vehicle, with insights on people movers and a recap test to assess the progress made in road safety knowledge and driving skills.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Treasure hunter Drew Pritchard travels the length and breadth of the country in search of elusive gems and other magnificent objects.

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Lifestyle brand Salt Life needs Wayde and Brett's help again. This time they want an aquarium even bigger and better than the original.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike hopes to capitalise on '80s nostalgia by restoring an original six-cylinder 1982 Toyota Supra with a tired interior, faulty head gasket, compromised exhaust system, and only one working pop-up headlight.

programme.title: Opal Hunters: Red Dirt Road Trip
programme.desc: Only a week is left before JC has to return home for the birth of his first child and in a final sprint to reach their new $20,000 target, Rod has jammed as many money-making schemes as possible into 7 days.

programme.title: The Grand Tour
programme.desc: Jeremy, Richard and James are told to take a holiday across the Southwestern United States in an RV. They stage a mutiny to get rid of the assigned ride and drive an RV of their making.

programme.title: Bush Wreck Rescue
programme.desc: Glen and Robin agree to a build-off competition, while Bill purchases a vintage Cadillac that appears to be in mint condition until he starts scraping off the paint.

programme.title: Drive Safer
programme.desc: In the final episode, the focus is on safety when purchasing your next vehicle, with insights on people movers and a recap test to assess the progress made in road safety knowledge and driving skills.

programme.title: Opal Hunters: Red Dirt Road Trip
programme.desc: Only a week is left before JC has to return home for the birth of his first child and in a final sprint to reach their new $20,000 target, Rod has jammed as many money-making schemes as possible into 7 days.

programme.title: The Grand Tour
programme.desc: Jeremy, Richard and James are told to take a holiday across the Southwestern United States in an RV. They stage a mutiny to get rid of the assigned ride and drive an RV of their making.

programme.desc: Glen and Robin agree to a build-off competition, while Bill purchases a vintage Cadillac that appears to be in mint condition until he starts scraping off the paint.

programme.sub-title: Gas Barbecues, Mattress Pads, and Ear Prostheses

programme.sub-title: Recycled Skateboards, Braided Pastry, Construction Trailers, and Vises
programme.desc: More everyday items are put under the microscope as we discover how skateboards are manufactured and braided pastry is produced.

programme.desc: In the final episode, the focus is on safety when purchasing your next vehicle, with insights on people movers and a recap test to assess the progress made in road safety knowledge and driving skills.
